_note: Routing, oneway, and alignment ground truth for scripts/verify-network.ts, the analogue of known-heights.json. Intersection coordinates are real OSM graph nodes verified to sit on the named corner. Route ground truth is the straight-line (single street) or Manhattan (one turn) geometric distance between the named corners, an independent geometric measure; the gate asserts the graph's routed Dijkstra distance reproduces it within tolerance. This catches scale errors (a Web Mercator 1.38x bug would blow the tolerance), missing edges (a gap forces a long detour), and gross topology faults. Oneway directions are the real downtown directions: Adelaide St E eastbound, Richmond St E westbound (the Richmond-Adelaide one-way pair). Alignment uses building coordinates from known-heights.json reprojected through the shared city origin; if road and buildings did not share one frame, the nearest road node would be far away.

routes.note: one block west on King St E (two-way)
one long block west on King St E (two-way)
one block south on Church St (two-way)
one block south on Jarvis St (two-way)
King St E east to Jarvis, then south to Front (Manhattan, one turn)

onewayChecks.name: Adelaide St E eastbound
onewayChecks.note: Adelaide St E is one-way eastbound; the eastbound edge must exist and the reverse must not

onewayChecks.name: Richmond St E westbound
onewayChecks.note: Richmond St E is one-way westbound; the westbound edge must exist and the reverse must not

alignmentChecks.name: L Tower vs nearest road node
